Well, the guy I'm speaking for, I know he ain't paying fees. You know, we're there on 50-50 split on product sales. This is a no-fee gig. So, I know I'm making money, but it's pretty obvious to me, he ain't making money. So, I said to Rodney, I said, how the hell is this guy making any money? You know? And Rodney said, he ain't. He said, you know, and by the way, Foster's whole stick is, you know, prosperity thinking. Well, yeah. And I mean, Rod said, he's probably making, you know, 50 Gs a year tops. And very popular in chiropractic. I mean, very well-known in chiropractic. So Foster stays as much as me speak. And I mean, it was a small audience. I was 100 doctors or something. But I mean, I got like 80 of them. And you know, I did like, this is years ago, I did maybe 40, 50,000 bucks. And so Foster corners me afterwards. And he says, let me talk to you. And he says, I want to do that. He said, and I'm no good at it. I said, yeah, I shouldn't. You know, I said, it's the most pitiful thing I've ever seen. So anyway, so we wound up building a business around them. And we created a tour forum. And we fixed them. And got to make a lot of money. But here was our leverage point for Foster. We were able to connect Foster to Napoleon Hill and attach him. Now, the actual truth of the story is that Napoleon Hill and three partners attempted to franchise. Napoleon Hill, like Dale Carnegie, was franchised. In their pilot operation in San Francisco, they hired Foster as the preview speaker to do the evening introductory seminars to sell people into the course. I turned that into, he mentioned it as an, oh, by the way, I turned it into the whole story. I mean, I didn't tell it the way I just told it to you. But I hooked him to Napoleon Hill at the hip. I worked closely with Napoleon Hill. Well, that is true. They did spend one day in a room, him and the three guys, closely. They all sat at the same table. And they worked on what the hell they were going to do to try and make this franchise work. So he did work closely with Napoleon Hill. I just left out the part that it was for about four and a half hours if you take out lunch. I didn't mention that part of the story. So the attachment instantly made him more valuable and more promotable and able to do better from the front of the room. So there's the strategy of attachment. This is relatively new. These are two guys. So think about now what Trump has done most recently. Again, to look at Trump, is Trump's connected himself to Kiyosaki. And Kiyosaki has connected himself to Trump. Well, why? Well, it's kind of obvious why Kiyosaki would connect himself to Trump, even though Kiyosaki's done very well, which poor dad's been on a New York Times bestseller list for I don't know how long. Three years, four years, five years, which is a feat beyond my ability to conceive, spawned the entire series of books. I mean, Robert's done very, very, very well with one short story, which is fundamentally what he's got. I had a rich dad. I had a poor dad. Rich dad did different things than poor dad did. That's it. I gave it a whole shtick. Just 512 books, six seminars, a game, DVDs. That's the story. It tells you the importance of a story. One good story. It's better to have 10, but one good story can make you a lot of money. But now Kiyosaki has connected himself to Trump. Even though Kiyosaki's already doing very, very well, that makes some sense. Trump's connected himself to Kiyosaki. Why? Same reason he's at World Wrestling Federation offering to shave off his hair. Why not? That's the main reason. Why? It's because there's a whole constituency over there that he might not reach were it not for that connection. There's no harm in doing so. It's a segment of the market that's different to him. So this is Mark Victor Hansen and our link letter. I'm only paying attention to it because we just got our link letter for our client, Miracle Air. The most common thing you hear when you talk to people about our link letter is he's still alive. And yeah, he is. He's 94. And he's doing great.
